compiled_state.title: stat -f %m macOS syntax causes crashes on Linux in 5 scripts
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: The stat -f %m pattern (macOS file modification time) is used in 5 scripts and causes unbound variable errors on Linux where stat -f means 'filesystem mode'. Affected files: plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh (line 71), plugins/dso/scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh, plugins/dso/scripts/health-check.sh, plugins/dso/hooks/track-cascade-failures.sh, plugins/dso/skills/ui-discover/lock.sh. On Linux, stat -f %m interprets %m as a filename, not a format specifier. With set -u (nounset), the unexpected stdout output triggers unbound variable errors in arithmetic contexts. Fix: use portable pattern: stat -c %Y file 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m file 2>/dev/null (Linux first, macOS fallback).

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=1, intermittent=0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Prior fix commit 47e1bfeb partially fixed this pattern but left 5+ files unfixed. System documented to require Linux portability.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing sprint-list-epics.sh, worktree-cleanup.sh, compute-diff-hash.sh, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md. Fix: uname-based stat detection.
comments.author: Test
